title: Linking Phrasal Verbs
learningGoal: Master the smooth linking sounds of phrasal verbs with pronouns (turn it on, take it off).
instructions: 句動詞と代名詞（it）が連続する時の「リンキング（音の繋がり）」を練習します。単語を区切らずに、一つの単語のように滑らかに発音してください。(Practice the linked pronunciation of phrasal verbs with pronouns. Say them smoothly as one word.)

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Link the 'n' to 'i', and 't' to 'o'. Tur-ni-ton.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'turn itto on' with pauses between words.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: The 't's become soft 'd' sounds in American English. Pu-di-don.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: それを解決する、理解する
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Fi-gyer-i-dout. The 't' in it links to the 'o' in out.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: とても簡単（イディオム）

targetPhrasesOrWords.transliteration: アンダー ザ ウェザー
targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: 体調が悪い（イディオム）
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Make sure your tongue is between your teeth for 'the' and 'wea-ther'.

expectedPronunciationText: turn it on turn it off take it off put it on give it up figure it out piece of cake under the weather wake up break a leg

feedbackRules.onPass: Excellent! Your linking sounds very natural. You sound like a native speaker!
feedbackRules.onFail: Please try again. Don't pause between the words. Try to say 'take it off' like one long word: 'taykitoff'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Remember to connect the consonant at the end of the verb to the 'i' in 'it'. 'Turn-it' sounds like 'Tur-nit'.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.exactCorrectTranscript: turn it on turn it off take it off put it on give it up figure it out piece of cake under the weather wake up break a leg
acceptedVariants: turn it on turn it off take it off put it on give it up figure it out piece of cake under the weather wake up break a leg
adminCorrectAnswerSet.nativeScriptReference: turn it on turn it off take it off put it on give it up figure it out piece of cake under the weather wake up break a leg
adminCorrectAnswerSet.transliteration: turniton turnitoff taykitoff pudidon givitup figyeridout peesuvcake unduhthehweather waykup braykuhleg

audioAssets.audioTranscript: turn it on, turn it off, take it off, put it on, give it up, figure it out, piece of cake, under the weather, wake up, break a leg

hintText: Read 'turn it on' as if it is spelled 'tur-ni-ton'. Don't stop your breath!
